milestone: Vihu Card Platform v1 -- Card Art (front/back visual, download, print)
slice: A real product gap, not a bug: the minted-card list showed only a text row, never an actual card artifact. A new View Card button opens a preview drawing a real front and back onto two <canvas> elements at print-resolution (700x980, poker-card ratio) using only real data -- card.pattern/constellation/code/rarity/label/createdAt plus the World's own name/icon/Hero-Image -- never fabricated illustration art or a fictional Creator credit. Front: full-bleed Hero-Image background (or a rarity-tinted gradient with the World icon when none exists), a Builder-Card type pill, rarity glyph, the World's own name and the Card's label, a bottom bar pairing a rarity-coloured pill with the real code. Back: a dark starfield showing the Card's own actual pattern coordinates connected star-to-star (pixel-sampled at a real seeded coordinate to confirm it isn't a placeholder), the constellation name, the human-readable code, and a tagline. Download reuses the existing _downloadDataURL() helper against each canvas's own toDataURL PNG. Print builds a temporary off-screen sheet with both images, sized to real 2.5in x 3.5in via a new @media print block, torn down after printing. Verified via a new 12-assertion Playwright suite plus the full existing 47-assertion Card Platform suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged. No schema/RPC/cardPlatform.js change -- purely additive World Builder UI. See docs/VIHU_CARD_PLATFORM.md and CLAUDE.md's matching entry.
